tasks.title: Collapse learning-schema-v2.json into canonical learning-schema.json and refresh stale contract doc
tasks.description: Root cause: `claude-code-shared/contracts/learning-schema-v2.json` was created as a NEW physical file rather than bumping the existing `learning-schema.json` in place, because the design framed unification as 'a v2 superset schema' in the seed (docs/seeds/20260605-1358-retro-attribution-learning.json — decisions line 17, implementation_decisions[0]) and to-tasks froze that framing into a literal new path in T-0026 of docs/tasks/20260605-1501-retro-attribution-learning.json. This was a deliberate instruction that flowed cleanly through seed -> to-tasks -> run-tasks (NOT context blowup). The new-physical-file convention only earns its keep when old v1 DATA must keep validating against v1 while new data uses v2; here the store has ZERO data (the seed states this is exactly why unification was 'near-free' now) and `scripts/log-learning.py` was switched wholesale to v2 (SCHEMA_PATH -> learning-schema-v2.json), so v1 is now orphaned. Version is ALREADY expressed by the internal `schema_version` const ('1' in v1, '2' in v2), so the `-v2` filename suffix redundantly double-encodes the version. Collateral left by the migration: (a) `learning-schema.json` (v1) is dead — referenced by no live code; (b) `contracts/learning-contract.md` still documents the PRE-migration world (per-skill `learnings/<skill>.jsonl` files, schema_version '1', the closed `trigger` enum vocabulary) that T-0029 already deleted. Failing scenario: `ls claude-code-shared/contracts/learning-schema*.json` shows TWO files; `grep -rn learning-schema-v2 claude-code-shared` shows the redundant suffix in 5 live files plus the schema's own $id; `learning-contract.md` describes a storage layout and trigger vocab that no longer exist. Test seam: no runtime seam — this is structural file-duplication, not a runtime behavior, so there is no pre-fix failing test (Phase 1 was correctly skipped via the complexity gate). Verified instead by the existing pytest suite staying green after reference updates plus validate-schema.sh validating a record against the renamed schema. Fix approach: (1) `git rm claude-code-shared/contracts/learning-schema.json` (delete dead v1). (2) `git mv claude-code-shared/contracts/learning-schema-v2.json claude-code-shared/contracts/learning-schema.json`. (3) Inside the renamed schema set `"$id": "learning-schema"` (was 'learning-schema-v2'); KEEP `schema_version` const '2' — that internal field is now the single source of truth for the version. (4) Update the 5 live references from `learning-schema-v2.json` -> `learning-schema.json`: `scripts/log-learning.py` (SCHEMA_PATH at line 38, plus docstring/error lines 6, 14, 123), `agents/attribution-tracer.md` (line 29), `agents/artifact-grounding-judge.md` (line 19), `contracts/learning-cause-vocabulary.json` (_comment line 2), `scripts/tests/test_log_learning_v2.py` (lines 1, 15); optionally `git mv` that test to `test_log_learning.py` to drop the lingering '_v2'. (5) Rewrite `contracts/learning-contract.md` to document the POST-migration reality: a single unified append-only log at `claude-code-shared/learnings/unified.jsonl` (NOT per-skill files), schema `contracts/learning-schema.json` schema_version '2', the v2 record shape (type self|attribution, reported_by, improves, improves_type, cause open-vocab + cause_label, problem, why_missed, lesson, fix, evidence, trace, confidence, status), the writer `scripts/log-learning.py` injecting schema_version/id/timestamp, the agents (capture-learning + learning-grounding-judge for self records; attribution-tracer + artifact-grounding-judge for attribution records), and the open `cause` vocabulary living in `learning-cause-vocabulary.json` (replacing the old closed `trigger` enum table). IMPORTANT — DO NOT TOUCH the hook chain: `hooks/stop-hook.py` and the debug/code-review SKILL.md tail blocks never reference the schema filename (the hook is marker-only; the tails reference agents by name), so they must remain unchanged. This is one coupled task — the rename and the doc rewrite must land together because the doc must point at the renamed file.
acceptance_criteria: No pre-fix failing test exists because the defect is structural file-duplication with zero runtime symptom; instead, after the change the existing suite passes (`python3 -m pytest claude-code-shared/scripts/tests/test_log_learning_v2.py -q`) and `bash claude-code-shared/scripts/validate-schema.sh --instance claude-code-shared/contracts/learning-schema.json <a minimal valid v2 record>` exits 0
Exactly one learning schema file exists at `claude-code-shared/contracts/learning-schema.json`; `learning-schema-v2.json` no longer exists and the old v1 schema was removed via `git rm`
`grep -rn 'learning-schema-v2' claude-code-shared` returns zero matches (filename, $id, and all live references updated)
The renamed schema keeps `schema_version` const '2' and its `$id` is `learning-schema`

All 5 live references resolve to the renamed file: `echo '<valid record>' | python3 claude-code-shared/scripts/log-learning.py` appends exactly one valid line to `claude-code-shared/learnings/unified.jsonl` with no schema-load error

`learning-contract.md` no longer references per-skill `<skill>.jsonl`, `schema_version "1"`, or the `trigger` enum table; it documents the unified `learnings/unified.jsonl` log, schema_version '2', the v2 record shape, and the open `cause` vocabulary (pointing at `learning-cause-vocabulary.json`)
`hooks/stop-hook.py` and the `skills/debug/SKILL.md` + `skills/code-review/SKILL.md` tail blocks are byte-for-byte unchanged (confirmed they never referenced the schema filename)
tasks.pr: gh pr create --title 'Collapse learning schema to canonical name, harden tracer contract' --base main

tasks.title: Harden attribution-tracer agent to enforce judge-routes-write contract
tasks.description: Root cause: the attribution-tracer agent bypassed the artifact-grounding-judge and wrote directly to `learnings/unified.jsonl` via `log-learning.py` on its first real use. The contract in `claude-code-shared/agents/attribution-tracer.md` does state the rule (Step 6: 'Spawn the artifact-grounding-judge agent'; What-you-must-not-do: 'Do not write to learnings/unified.jsonl directly — that is the judge's job after verification'), but the instructions are not explicit about the tool mechanism. Step 6 says to 'Spawn the artifact-grounding-judge agent' without specifying the Agent tool invocation with `subagent_type: artifact-grounding-judge`. The agent resolved the ambiguity by calling `log-learning.py` itself instead. The two-agent split (tracer drafts, judge writes) is the system's core integrity guarantee: the verifier must never grade its own draft. This fix must close the ambiguity so the mechanism is unambiguous. Fix approach: (1) Rewrite Step 6 in `claude-code-shared/agents/attribution-tracer.md` to specify the exact Agent tool invocation: 'Use the Agent tool with subagent_type: artifact-grounding-judge and pass the draft record in the prompt body. The judge verifies anchors against artifacts and calls log-learning.py only if grounded.' (2) Move the 'Do not write directly' prohibition UP to be the first item in the 'What you must not do' section, and add a rationale: 'Calling log-learning.py directly bypasses grounding verification and self-grades the record. Only the artifact-grounding-judge writes.' (3) Add an explicit line to the final step: 'Your final action is spawning the artifact-grounding-judge. You do not call log-learning.py. You do not report a write. The judge's verdict and write are its own responsibility.' (4) Check `claude-code-shared/agents/artifact-grounding-judge.md` — confirm it similarly does NOT instruct the attribution-tracer to call log-learning.py, and that its own 'on pass, call log-learning.py' instruction is present and unambiguous. No changes to the debug or code-review SKILL.md attribution-capture tail blocks — they spawn attribution-tracer by name, and the tracer's own hardening is sufficient.
acceptance_criteria: Step 6 in `claude-code-shared/agents/attribution-tracer.md` specifies 'Use the Agent tool with subagent_type: artifact-grounding-judge' (exact phrase) rather than generic 'spawn'

The 'Do not write to learnings/unified.jsonl directly' prohibition appears as the FIRST bullet in the 'What you must not do' section with a rationale sentence explaining why (bypasses grounding, self-grades the record)
A sentence in Step 6 explicitly states the tracer's final action is spawning the judge and that the tracer does NOT call log-learning.py
`claude-code-shared/agents/artifact-grounding-judge.md` retains its 'on pass, call log-learning.py' instruction unchanged (confirmed present — no regression)
The debug and code-review SKILL.md attribution-capture tail blocks are byte-for-byte unchanged (confirmed — the fix is in the tracer agent only)

follow_ups.title: Debug cleanup and post-mortem
steps: Run the full infra test suite and record the baseline: `python3 -m pytest claude-code-shared/scripts/tests/ -q`
Confirm no debug instrumentation was added this session: `grep -rn '\[DEBUG-' claude-code-shared` — expect zero matches (Phase 3 instrumentation was skipped via the complexity gate)
Re-run the suite after T-0035 and confirm no new failures versus the baseline
Confirm no throwaway harness files were created this session (none expected): check for `*.scratch.*` or `test-harness.*`
No browser-check run dirs were created (this was a non-UI structural fix); nothing to delete under docs/browser-checks

State the winning root cause in the PR description: the `learning-schema-v2.json` filename was a deliberate seed/to-tasks framing choice (not context blowup), redundant against the internal `schema_version` const, which left the v1 schema orphaned and `learning-contract.md` stale; the fix collapses to one canonically-named file with the version tracked solely by the internal const
